input.cmd:
# Measure latency distribution under load
for i in $(seq 1 50); do curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{time_total}\n" http://web:8080/orders; done | sort -n | awk 'BEGIN{c=0} {a[c++]=$1} END{print "n="c; print "p50="a[int(c*0.5)]; print "p90="a[int(c*0.9)]; print "p99="a[int(c*0.99)]; print "max="a[c-1]}'

# Check all endpoints and payments service
curl -s http://web:8080/ 2>&1; echo
curl -s http://payments:8080/ 2>&1; echo
curl -s http://sreft-fa-payments:8080/ 2>&1; echo
docker exec sreft-fa-operator sh -c 'getent hosts payments; getent hosts load; curl -s -w "
%{time_total}
" http://payments:8080/ 2>&1; curl -s -w "
%{time_total}
" http://payments:8080/charge 2>&1; curl -s -w "
%{time_total}
" http://payments:8080/pay 2>&1'

input.cmd:
# Concurrent load test - maybe p99 spikes under concurrency
# Also check if load generator is actually seeing latency
docker logs sreft-fa-load 2>&1
# Try higher concurrency
for i in $(seq 1 20); do curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{time_total}\n" http://web:8080/orders & done; wait
echo done
# Check if there's a /slow or other endpoints
for path in / /orders /health /ready /metrics /status /api /checkout /pay /payment /latency /debug /stats; do
  code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://web:8080$path)
  echo "$path -> $code"
done

# Look at compose file path - maybe accessible via host mount somehow
# The path is on Mac host: /var/folders/...
# Check docker compose project
docker compose -p sreft-false-alarm ps 2>&1
# Try to find compose file
find / -name 'docker-compose.yaml' 2>/dev/null
# Check if host path is mounted anywhere
docker inspect sreft-fa-operator --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
docker inspect sreft-web --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
docker inspect sreft-fa-load --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
